patterns: What do I need to enroll?
What documents do I need to submit?
What are the requirements for incoming freshmen?
What do I need to get in?
What do I need for CvSU?
What's required to enroll at CvSU?
responses:
For freshman admission to CvSU, you will need:
1. Duly accomplished CvSU Application Form
2. Original Form 138 (Grade 12 Report Card)
3. Certificate of Good Moral Character
4. PSA Birth Certificate (photocopy)
5. 2x2 ID pictures (2 copies)
6. CvSU Entrance Examination qualifying score

Please visit the Admissions Office or go to https://www.cvsu.edu.ph for the latest details.

patterns: How to register for the entrance exam?
How do I take the entrance exam?
What do I need to apply?
How can I apply to CvSU?
responses: The CvSU College Admission Test (CVSUCAT) is typically held from October to January for incoming first-year students. You must register online through the Official CvSU Admissions portal. Check https://www.cvsu.edu.ph for the current schedule and online registration link.
CVSUCAT (CvSU College Admission Test) is conducted annually. Registration is done online. Visit https://www.cvsu.edu.ph for the exact schedule and guidelines.

patterns: How do I enroll at CvSU?
responses:
CvSU Enrollment Steps:
1. Log in to the CvSU Student Portal
2. Verify your admission status
3. Proceed to online registration/pre-enlistment
4. Pay tuition and fees at the Cashier's Office or via online payment channels
5. Present proof of payment to the Registrar
6. Receive your Certificate of Registration (COR)

For full details, visit the Registrar's Office or the official CvSU website.

patterns: When is the enrollment for this semester?
responses: CvSU holds enrollment at the start of each semester. First semester enrollment is usually in July–August, and second semester enrollment is in December–January. Please check the official CvSU Academic Calendar at https://cvsu.edu.ph for the exact dates.

patterns: What can I study at CvSU?
What are the programs at CvSU?
What college programs does CvSU have?
Can I study engineering at CvSU?
responses:
CvSU offers a wide range of undergraduate and graduate programs across several colleges, in fields including Agriculture, Arts and Sciences, Engineering and Information Technology, Education, Business/Management, Veterinary Medicine, and others.

Examples of popular programs: BS Computer Science, BSIT, BS Agriculture, BS Education, and various engineering and business programs. The exact list of colleges and programs differs by campus and may be updated over time.

For the official, current list of CvSU colleges and the programs offered at each campus, please visit https://www.cvsu.edu.ph.

patterns: Is there Computer Science at CvSU?
responses:
CvSU's College of Engineering and Information Technology (CEIT) typically offers programs such as:

• BS Computer Science (BSCS)
• BS Information Technology (BSIT)
• BS Computer Engineering (BSCpE)
• BS Electronics Engineering (BSECE)
• BS Civil Engineering (BSCE)
• BS Agricultural Engineering (BSAE)

Program availability may vary per campus and academic year. For the current list of CEIT programs, the campuses that offer each, and the latest accreditation status, please contact CEIT or check https://www.cvsu.edu.ph.

CvSU offers graduate programs through its Graduate School, which may include programs such as:

• Master of Arts in Education (MAEd)
• Master of Science in Agriculture (MS Agriculture)
• Master of Business Administration (MBA)
• Master of Science programs in selected fields
• Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in selected fields

For the current, complete list of master's and doctoral programs and their requirements, please contact the CvSU Graduate School Office or visit https://www.cvsu.edu.ph.

patterns: How much does it cost to study at CvSU?
How much is the tuition per semester?
Do I have to pay for CvSU?
responses:
CvSU is a state university, so Filipino students enjoy highly subsidized tuition fees. Under the Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act (RA 10931 / Free Higher Education Law), qualified students may study FREE of tuition and other school fees.

However, some miscellaneous fees may still apply depending on program and campus. Please contact the Cashier's Office or visit https://www.cvsu.edu.ph for the exact fee schedule per program.

patterns: How can I get financial aid?
responses:
CvSU students can avail of various scholarship programs:

1. UniFAST / Free Higher Education (RA 10931)
2. CHED Merit Scholarship
3. DOST-SEI Scholarship
4. PESFA (Private Education Student Financial Assistance)
5. CvSU Institutional Scholarships
6. Local Government Unit (LGU) Scholarships

Visit the CvSU Scholarship & Financial Assistance Office (SFAO) for eligibility and application details.

patterns: How do I get to the campus?
responses:
The CvSU Main Campus (Don Severino delas Alas Campus) is located in Indang, Cavite, Philippines.

CvSU operates additional campuses across the province of Cavite. For the verified, official list of all CvSU campuses and their addresses, please refer to https://www.cvsu.edu.ph — I want to avoid sharing an outdated or inaccurate roster of campus locations.

The Main Campus can typically be reached via jeepney from Dasmariñas or Tagaytay; verify current routes locally.

patterns: How do I get to the campus?
responses:
The CvSU Main Campus typically offers facilities such as:

• University Library (physical and digital resources)
• Sports/athletic facilities
• Student dormitory (for qualified students; availability varies)
• Canteen / food services
• Computer Laboratories
• Science and Research Laboratories
• University Health / clinic services
• Chapel
• Auditorium

Specialized facilities (e.g., agricultural training areas, veterinary facilities) reflect CvSU's programs, but specific facilities vary per campus. For the verified current list of facilities at your campus of interest, please check https://www.cvsu.edu.ph.

patterns: How do I access the library?
responses: The CvSU Main Library offers physical and online resources, including e-books and online journals through the CvSU Library portal. Library hours and access policies vary by campus and academic period. For the current schedule, services, and the correct library contact for your campus, please check https://www.cvsu.edu.ph or your campus library directly.

CvSU regularly holds a variety of events through the academic year, which may include:

• CvSU Foundation / Charter Anniversary
• University Sportsfest
• Research Symposium
• Cultural Night
• Job and Career Fair
• Semestral orientation for freshmen
• Science and Technology Week

Exact dates and the specific lineup change each academic year. For the latest official events and announcements, please follow the verified CvSU Facebook page and check https://www.cvsu.edu.ph.

patterns: When is the first day of classes?
responses:
CvSU follows the CHED academic calendar. Typically:
• First Semester: August – December
• Second Semester: January – May
• Summer: June – July (for certain programs)

The latest Academic Calendar is published on the official CvSU website at https://www.cvsu.edu.ph.

patterns: How do I contact the admissions office?
responses:
You can reach CvSU through:

• Official website: https://www.cvsu.edu.ph (current directory of phone numbers and emails)
• General email: info@cvsu.edu.ph
• Admissions Office: admissions@cvsu.edu.ph
• Registrar's Office: registrar@cvsu.edu.ph (verify the exact address per campus)
• Official Facebook page: linked from https://www.cvsu.edu.ph (be cautious of impostor pages)

For verified phone numbers and the current office hours of the campus or office you need, please refer to the official campus directory on the CvSU website.

The CvSU Registrar's Office handles:
• Transcript of Records (TOR)
• Certificate of Enrollment/Registration
• Diploma and Honors Certificates
• Honorable Dismissal
• Transfer Credentials
• Graduation clearance

For the verified registrar contact details (email, phone, and office hours) at your campus, please check https://www.cvsu.edu.ph.

patterns: What kind of school is CvSU?
responses:
Cavite State University (CvSU) is a state university in the Philippines, with its Main Campus in Indang, Cavite. Its origins date back to 1906, and it was granted university status in 1998 under Republic Act No. 8468.

CvSU is known for its programs in Agriculture, Engineering, Education, and Technology, among other fields.

Motto: 'Truth, Excellence, Service'
Tagline: 'Iskolar para sa Bayan'

For the official vision, mission, and full institutional profile, please refer to https://www.cvsu.edu.ph.

patterns: What is the mission of CvSU?
responses: For the official CvSU Vision, Mission, Core Values, and Goals — which the university periodically updates — please refer to the institutional profile on https://www.cvsu.edu.ph. The CvSU motto is 'Truth, Excellence, Service' and its tagline is 'Iskolar para sa Bayan.' I'm pointing you to the official source rather than paraphrasing the formal Vision/Mission statements, so you get the exact, current wording.

CvSU has an active student life with various recognized organizations, typically including:

• A university-wide student government
• College-based student councils
• Academic and professional organizations
• Cultural and arts groups
• Sports clubs and varsity teams
• Religious organizations
• Service-oriented clubs

The exact list of recognized organizations changes over time. Visit the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS) at your campus, or check https://www.cvsu.edu.ph, to see the current list and how to join.
